messages.locale.label: Language
messages.hero.title: Intelligent Rule Manager
messages.hero.lede: A cross-platform desktop app for browsing, editing, composing, and exporting Markdown rule libraries backed by a shared Rust core and CLI.
messages.tabs.label: Workspace views
messages.status.runtimeValue: Running in {mode} mode with data from {layer}.
messages.status.storageLoading: Loading storage root from rule-core workspace summary...
messages.insights.label: Recommendation insights
messages.workspace.label: Rule manager workspace
messages.panel.library.title: Library
messages.panel.library.body: Search, group, tag, and target filters make the file-backed rule store navigable.
messages.panel.workspace.title: Workspace
messages.panel.workspace.body: Create new rules, edit body content, and review a live preview before saving.
messages.panel.inspector.title: Inspector
messages.panel.inspector.body: Metadata editing stays visible while the body editor remains in the main workspace.
messages.overview.flowBody: Move from discovery to editing to composition without leaving the same workspace shell.
messages.overview.selectionBody: Keep one rule in focus so editing, previewing, and composition stay aligned.
messages.overview.metricsBody: Track rule volume, taxonomy breadth, and composition readiness from one sidebar summary.
messages.library.resultsBody: Use filters to narrow the shared rule library, then inspect one rule deeply before editing or composing it.
messages.search.label: Search rules
messages.search.placeholder: Search by title, id, tag, or group
messages.empty.loadingWorkspaceBody: Fetching the current rule library summary and list.
messages.empty.noMatchingRulesBody: Try a broader search term or reset one of the active filters.
messages.empty.noRulesBody: Create Markdown rule files in the resolved rule storage root to populate this list.
messages.empty.noDocumentBody: Select a rule to edit its body or open the new-rule form.
messages.empty.noRuleSelectedBody: Select a rule from the library pane to inspect and edit it here.
messages.create.title: Create rule from template
messages.create.summaryPlaceholder: One sentence explaining the purpose of the rule.
messages.compose.selectionBody: Choose tags, explicit rules, or both. The final export includes the union of those matching rules.
messages.compose.exportBody: Generate one AGENTS.md bundle or a split Trae rules bundle from the selected rules.
messages.compose.stageSelectBody: Start with broad tags, then tighten the bundle with explicit rule picks.
messages.compose.stageAssembleBody: Review the matched bundle, choose a target, then export and preview the generated files.
messages.compose.noSelectionBody: Select at least one tag or one rule to generate an export bundle.
messages.compose.previewEmpty: Run a compose export to preview the generated content here.
messages.compose.tagHint: Tag matches also include ancestor-tag filtering such as web -> typescript.
messages.compose.ruleHint: Explicit rule selection is added on top of tag-based matches.
messages.milestones.label: Next milestones
messages.milestone.composeExport: Implement rule compose and export actions from the shared Rust core
messages.milestone.previewPolish: Add validation polish and richer markdown preview rendering
messages.milestone.smokeTest: Run a full Tauri smoke test against the desktop shell
messages.browser.previewRuleSummary: Fallback example shown when the app is running outside Tauri.
messages.browser.previewRuleIntent: Preview how the desktop app behaves before the Tauri runtime is attached.
messages.browser.previewRuleBody: This sample rule lets the browser build stay interactive during local UI work.
messages.browser.newRuleIntent: State the scenario or goal this rule is meant to support.
messages.browser.newRuleBody: Write the rule in normal Markdown so it stays compatible with AGENTS-style documents and Trae rule files.
messages.browser.ruleNotFound: No browser fallback rule found for {file}

reasons: The current library is still small enough that a CLI-first workflow is efficient.
suggestedFeatures: Keep using Markdown files plus the CLI for assembly and export.

reasons: The rule library is growing and visual browsing will soon save review time.
suggestedFeatures: Add stronger compose and inspection flows before the library becomes harder to navigate.

reasons: The rule library is large enough that desktop filtering, editing, and previews should be first-class.
suggestedFeatures: Prioritize native-feeling composition, editing, and export workflows in the desktop client.

cli.cli.about: CLI for the intelligent rule manager workspace
cli.arg.locale.help: Choose the CLI locale, for example en or zh-CN
cli.command.healthcheck.about: Print the shared rule-core healthcheck as JSON
cli.command.workspace_summary.about: Print the current shared rules workspace summary as JSON
cli.command.stats.about: Print rule library statistics as JSON
cli.command.recommend.about: Print the current visualization recommendation as JSON
cli.command.inspect.about: Inspect one rule document as JSON
cli.command.create.about: Create one rule document and print it as JSON
cli.command.compose.about: Compose selected rules into AGENTS.md or split Trae exports
cli.arg.file.help: Path to the rule Markdown file
cli.arg.target.help: Compose target, for example agents-md or trae-rule

package.extension.description: VS Code-compatible extension for opening and operating the Intelligent Rule Manager workspace.
